setting.note: The poet Ka comes back from years of exile in Germany to Kars, a poor, half-forgotten city in the far north-east of Turkey near the Armenian border, to report on an epidemic of suicides among devout young women, and is snowed in for three days during which a small theatrical coup convulses the town. The real Kars gives the book its bones: the snowbound streets, the theatre where the coup is broadcast live, the tea houses of the unemployed, and the ruined Armenian churches that speak of a vanished past.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Orhan Pamuk, first published 2002 (Kar). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Snow, Orhan Pamuk (2002). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cold blue-grey of a city sealed under snow, the weather that gives the book its title and its shape, each of Ka's poems a point on a snowflake. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
